text: Which of the following high-risk shipboard work activities strictly requires the issuance of a formal Permit to Work?

options.text: Working aloft on the mast or funnel structures
Working on live, high-voltage electrical circuits
Working outboard over the side of the hull structure

explanation: Working aloft, working on high-voltage circuits, and working outboard are all high-risk activities where the consequences of an error are severe. A permit-to-work system ensures these tasks are supervised and safely executed.
